title: ONE DAY: REBECCA CAMACHO PRESENTS | PLATFORM
content: The gallerist behind her titular space takes us through one day in San Francisco, from morning tea to her latest bedtime read. This is my favorite mug–my daughter made it at sleep-away camp this summer and my tea tastes so much better in it. A visit to my framers at Sterling Art Services to make presentation decisions for Forrest Kirk’s January exhibition of fantastic new unstretched paintings. Visits to the shop always last at least 45 minutes because we like to chat! I heard the first section of "We Were Three" on a *This American Life* episode and now I’m finishing the series. It’s sad and a little frustrating, but human and beautiful. A visit to the Legion of Honor always begins by admiring the view. I think it’s really important to be present for the spaces in your own community. A special gallery walkthrough with Jennie Ottinger and board members and staff of the Headlands Center for the Arts. I read to end my day.
